Migrate e2e tests from Cypress to Playwright (#865)

* Initial plan

* feat: migrate e2e tests from Cypress to Playwright

- Replace @nx/cypress with @nx/playwright executor
- Add @playwright/test and @nx/playwright dependencies
- Remove cypress, cypress-file-upload, eslint-plugin-cypress dependencies
- Move playwright from production to dev dependencies
- Create playwright.config.ts with NX preset
- Migrate app.cy.ts to app.spec.ts (Playwright)
- Migrate player.cy.ts to player.spec.ts (Playwright)
- Use page.addInitScript() for File System API mocking instead of
  complex Cypress commands with IndexedDB interception hack
- Move test fixtures from cypress/fixtures to e2e/fixtures
- Update project.json, nx.json, eslint config, and tsconfig
- Update .gitignore for Playwright artifacts

commit.message:
@wordpress/ui: add Collapsible component (#76280)

* Add Collapsible component to @wordpress/ui

Thin wrapper around Base UI's Collapsible component with Root,
Trigger, and Panel subcomponents. No custom styles — this is a
pure pass-through of the Base UI API.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add Storybook stories for Collapsible

Covers default, default-open, disabled, and controlled variants.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add unit tests for Collapsible

Covers ref forwarding, uncontrolled/controlled open state,
disabled behavior, and className pass-through.

Made-with: Cursor

* Update CHANGELOG for Collapsible component

Made-with: Cursor

* Address review feedback

- Fix import ordering: external (@base-ui) before @wordpress
- Add render prop tests for Root, Trigger, and Panel

Made-with: Cursor

* Update CollapsibleCard to use internal Collapsible wrapper

Replace direct @base-ui/react/collapsible imports with the new
internal Collapsible component in all three CollapsibleCard files.

In header.tsx, derive the open state from the aria-expanded prop
instead of the Base UI state callback, since our wrapper's render
prop type uses a single-argument signature.

Made-with: Cursor

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment explaining aria-expanded tradeoff in header render prop

The Collapsible wrapper's ComponentProps utility narrows the render
callback to a single-argument signature, so Base UI's `state` object
isn't available. Document why we read open state from `aria-expanded`.

Made-with: Cursor

* avoid unnecessary destructuring

* add hidden until found example

---------

Co-authored-by: ciampo <mciampini@git.wordpress.org>
Co-authored-by: mirka <0mirka00@git.wordpress.org>

by integration' on pull requests.

commit.message:
feat(ms1): 3D-Sonnenuhr mit Orientierung und Solarphysik

- Ausrichtungsmodus eingeführt: Automatisch/Nord oben/Süd oben
- Solarposition lokal berechnet (Sonnenhöhe/Azimut, NOAA-Näherung)
- Schattenlänge aus Sonnenhöhe (1/tan) abgeleitet
- Wallpaper-Rendering auf perspektivische Ellipsenplatte umgestellt
- Tages-Hemisphäre visuell hervorgehoben statt reinem Vollkreisfokus
- Geneigten Gnomon und physikalischen Schatten implementiert
- Konfigurationsdialog um Ausrichtung, Begründung und 3D-Toggle erweitert
- Unit-Tests in SundialCalculator erweitert (30/30 bestanden)
- M1-relevante Dokumentation aktualisiert

Bewusst nicht umgesetzt: M2 Adresssuche, M3 Instrumentenmodus/Atmosphäre

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: security hardening and code review fixes (v2.30.7)

- Fix reflected XSS in HA auth page via json.dumps escaping + URL scheme validation
- Fix adapter command injection with validate_adapter() regex in 5 bluetoothctl endpoints
- Add CSRF protection to all login forms (per-session token, hmac.compare_digest)
- Add Content-Security-Policy and X-Content-Type-Options security headers
- Fix MA monitor event loss by logging non-matching WebSocket messages
- Fix thread-safety in mDNS discovery (run_coroutine_threadsafe vs ensure_future)
- Fix volume read race condition with atomic lock scope
- Fix get_scan_job returning mutable reference instead of copy
- Sanitize 18 error responses to hide internal details (str(e) → generic messages)
- Add config upload size limit (1 MB)
- Remove 8 dead regex patterns from routes/api.py
- Fix DEFAULT_CONFIG shallow copy with deepcopy
- Fix deprecated asyncio.get_event_loop() → get_running_loop()
- Add prompt MA monitor stop() via websocket close
- Add pytest job to CI pipeline
- Pin dependency upper bounds (zeroconf<1.0, ruff<1.0, mypy<2.0)
- Add 29 new tests (adapter validation, XSS, CSRF)
- Update changelogs and history docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat!: inline plugin skills config, workspace schema v2 (#233) (#236)

BREAKING CHANGE: skill selection is now configured inline on each
plugin entry instead of top-level disabledSkills/enabledSkills arrays.

- Add PluginSkillsConfig type: string[] (allowlist) or {exclude:string[]} (blocklist)
- Add skills field to PluginEntrySchema object variant
- Add version field to WorkspaceConfigSchema
- Update getAllSkillsFromPlugins and collectPluginSkills to read inline skills
- Add pluginSkillsMode to SkillInfo for caller convenience
- Update workspace-modify and user-workspace write functions to write inline
- Simplify plugin-skills CLI: remove inAllowlistMode detection, use SkillInfo.pluginSkillsMode
- Add migrateWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 / migrateUserWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 called on sync
- Bump to v1.0.0, add CHANGELOG.md

Migration: automatic on next workspace sync. No manual action required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: auto-wrap flat SKILL.md repos as skills (#232) (#234)

When a plugin has no skills/ subdirectory, scan the plugin root for
directories containing SKILL.md and treat each as a skill. This gives
compatibility with the npx skills ecosystem (e.g. vercel-labs/agent-skills)
without any schema changes.

The enabledSkills/disabledSkills schema continues to work unchanged
since the plugin is still tracked as a workspace plugin entry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: allagents
repository.description: CLI tool for managing multi-repo AI agent workspaces with plugin synchronization across multiple AI clients.

commit.message:
feat: support installing skills individually (#228) (#231)

- Fix plugin install --skill when plugin already installed: detect
  'Plugin already exists' error and if --skill flags provided, skip
  the install step and proceed directly to enabling skills + sync

- Add top-level `allagents skills` command as shorthand alias for
  `allagents plugin skills` (list/add/remove), giving UX parity
  with `npx skills`

- Enhance `skills add` with --from/-f option: if skill not found in
  installed plugins, installs the specified plugin source first then
  enables the skill (mirrors `npx skills add <pkg> --skill <name>`)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: optimize _execOverDatabase query execution (#8059)

* Initial plan

* perf: optimize findByIds in _execOverDatabase - skip eventBulks$ subscription and return RxDocumentData[] directly

Two optimizations for the serial-find-by-id path:
1. Skip eventBulks$ subscribe/unsubscribe for findByIds (reduces RxJS overhead)
2. Return RxDocumentData[] instead of Map<string, RxDocument> (avoids double doc-cache processing and Map-to-Array-to-Map conversion)

Performance improvement (serial-find-by-id-50, memory storage, 15 runs):
- Before: median 2.45ms, average 2.46ms
- After:  median 1.68ms, average 1.78ms
- ~31% median improvement, ~28% average improvement

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: use _changeEventBuffer counter instead of eventBulks$ subscription for all query types

Replace the eventBulks$ subscribe/unsubscribe approach with a counter
snapshot for detecting concurrent writes during query execution.
This extends the performance benefit to count and find queries too.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: optimize memory storage query() and count() for find-by-query (#8058)

* Initial plan

* perf: optimize memory storage query() and count() methods

- query(): Add fast path when selector is satisfied by index - use pre-allocated
  array with indexed assignment instead of per-doc push with matcher checks.
  Skip unnecessary rows.slice() when no skip/limit is applied.
- count(): Compute count directly from index range bounds when selector is
  satisfied by index, avoiding full document array allocation.
- Clean up redundant `upperBound = upperBound` assignment.

Performance results (10 runs, 3000 docs):
  find-by-query:          2.731ms -> 2.427ms (11% faster)
  find-by-query-parallel: 3.507ms -> 3.376ms (4% faster)
  4x-count:               1.211ms -> 0.303ms (75% faster)

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use newRxError instead of throw new Error in count() method

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve type definitions: fix bug, document password typing, narrow types (#8056)

* Initial plan

* fix: improve type definitions across codebase

- Fix undefined `context` variable bug in sqlite-storage-instance.ts findDocumentsById
- Narrow `password` field from `string | any` (effectively `any`) to `string` in RxDatabaseCreator, RxStorageInstanceCreationParams, RxErrorParameters, and RxDatabaseBase
- Replace `type: 'object' | string` with `type: 'object'` in RxJsonSchema (old TypeScript workaround no longer needed)
- Address RxDumpCollectionAsAny TODO: change from `{ [P in keyof T]: any }` to `T` (preserves original property types)
- Improve `_saveData` oldData parameter from `any` to `RxDocumentData<RxDocType>`
- Remove outdated comments about encrypted properties being typed as any

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert password type narrowing and add comment explaining why it is typed as any

Different encryption plugins may use passwords that are not strings,
so the password field must remain typed as `string | any`.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert RxJsonSchema.type narrowing to fix CI type errors

The 'object' | string type is still needed because TypeScript infers
inline object literals' type field as string, not the literal 'object'.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use player_id instead of player_name for MA group lookup

get_ma_group_for_player() is keyed by player_id (UUID), but was called
with player_name (human-readable string), causing the lookup to always
return None. This made ma_group_play() never execute, so play after
pause only resumed one subprocess via fallback instead of the whole
MA syncgroup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
feat: periodic syncgroup refresh and atomic now-playing replacement

- Add _refresh_groups_via_ws() to rebuild syncgroup cache via WS
  players/all query every 60s and on player_updated events
- Include fuzzy name matching fallback for unmatched bridge players
- Replace per-entry set_ma_now_playing_for_group() with atomic
  replace_ma_now_playing() to clear stale entries
- Add replace_ma_now_playing() to state.py with lock-safe clear+update

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Make full dashboard cards clickable: Pregled svih zgrada & Potrebna pažnja (#539)

* Initial plan

* feat: make full dashboard cards clickable for Pregled svih zgrada and Potrebna pažnja

- Expand hit area of buildings overview card to full section (incl. padding)
  by moving click handler from dashBldToggleBtn to dashBldOverview section;
  guard prevents panel-content clicks from re-triggering the toggle.
- Add cursor:pointer + module-card-style hover state to .dashBldOverview.
- Attention items with data-dash-att-page now get role="button" + tabindex="0"
  so they are keyboard-focusable.
- Add keydown (Enter/Space) handler for attention items, consistent with the
  existing module-cards keydown pattern.
- Add :focus-visible outline to .dashAttItem for keyboard focus visibility.
